Example 	of 	the 	Month
Bill Glazer S %%S & D" (swipe and deploy)
Example 	of 	the 	Month
This Top Secret sealed "S & D" packet with your newsletter
contains a complete "Swipe <& Deploy" Sample of a tested, proven,
^ 	u n i v e r s a l l y 	a p p l i c a b l e 	m a r k e t i n g 	i t e m -
one month it may be a direct mail piece, another something to use at a website, another, a seasonal
program, another a complete tele-seminar or webinar campaign, etc. etc.
Our goal is to get as close to handing you a Turn-key, Ready-To-Use Marketing/Promotional Tool as is
possible, given there are literally thousands of different kinds of businesses making up Glazer-Kennedy
Insider's Circle™ membership. To anyone who uses just one of these a year, this at least QUADRUPLES THE
VALUE OF MEMBERSHIP, and makes every month a quick-to-moneymaking-action month.
IMPORTANT 	NOTE: 	These 	are 	intended 	ONLY 	for 	use 	of 	GKIC 	Members. 	Further,
whenever 	possible, 	helpful 	vendors, 	suppliers 	or 	facilitators 	will 	be 	provided 	should 	you
wish 	to 	use 	them.
%%
Scratch 	- 	It
Everyone Wants To Be A Winner!!!
n

-- 1 of 7 --

Why 	Use 	Promotions 	Of 	Chance?...
Just about everybody loves a contest-
Come on, they're fun! People really enjoy participating in them. And of course, it gets them into the
anticipation of WINNING!!!
I've found "games-of-chance" to be HUGELY successful - specifically where no skills are required, but the
anticipation of winning remains high.
"Scratch Off" mailings are a super form of client 'gaming' whether it's to reveal a Prize or a Secret Savings.
It's important to remember that the "game" alone is not enough. You want to tie it into something else that
leads to building your business such as:
• 	An 	appointment
• Another offer of your product or service
• A list building strategy
• A lead
• 	Etc.
For "Scratch Off" promotions, I've found it best to use the INSTANT SAVINGS/REWARD type. It's a format
where your customer/prospect can instantly reveal what they've won or will receive withOUT having to take
another action (like visiting your place of business, picking up the phone and calling you, or going to a website) *
first.
Included inside this month's "S & D" envelope, you have an actual example of a very successful promotion
used by HULEN Health Club:
There are a couple of key elements to pay attention to on this card to employ when structuring your
promotions:
• Choice of Reward, instead of just a single reward. This gets the recipient involved with the contest to
see if they've won.
• No requirement to buy, but you can only claim your gift by visiting the facility. Now it's up to the
marketing funnel to close the sale. The 'smart' entrepreneur will realize that this is now a numbers
game where a % of the people will not buy and another % will, depending on the skills of the sales
people.
NOTE: Non buyers should be placed on a continual "hot prospect" follow-up campaign.
• Clear and easy to understand way to respond (i.e. in this case...call)
• Photos that tell a story
• STRONG DEADLINE....48-Hours to respond or another winner will be selected {Bravo for having the
guts to do this)
• 	M a p
• Bullets of services offered
'-•

-- 2 of 7 --

Helpful Additional Information:
• Response is typically 50% Greater than withOUT the "Scratch-Off" component
• When used repeatedly in a close time-frame it will typically lose the impact. So don't overuse.
• Response is typically the greatest in the first 3-days the mailing promotion is received, so I would
suggest a relatively short deadline.
Below are samples of 2 other successful "Scratch-It" promotions. One that I used for my own Menswear store
the other for an Athletic Club, but these can (and should) be used for every type of business.
For 3-D.i,; Only, it's our...

Hay 3rd to Nay 6th - with this card only

-- 3 of 7 --

BONUS: Here, another clever way to use "Scratch-
It" 	promotions: K
Instead of just mailing them, make them up and hand them to customers when they visit your business or you
go to them.
For example:
The Auto-Repair shop could hand them to customers when they visit their shops to encourage them to
get all necessary repairs handled.
- The Insurance Agent could hand them out to prospects as a cool gift for them when requesting a quote
The Dentist could hand them to patients to see what extra gift they've won for giving them a name for
a referral
- Any business could implement a "Scratch-Off" on a door hanger
"S <& D" RESOURCE:
Enclosed is a Fax Back Form with TWO SPECIAL "GKIC Offers:"
• 1/2 hour "one-on-one" consultation with the team at Handy Mailing Service to determine how to best
use "Scratch-it" promotions for your business
• Special pricing for a Turn-Key (art, print, mail) campaign.
